feed.title: [Proposal] Public Fraud Proofs for Just-in-Time Channels

entry.title: [Proposal] Public Fraud Proofs for Just-in-Time Channels
entry.summary: The discussion about the UTXO commitment highlights its role as a temporary substitute for the funding transaction, which should be sent after the `funding_signed` step. Recognizing this crucial sequence, there is an intention to revise the associated paper to reflect these insights accurately. Following the feedback received, a new version of the document has been prepared and uploaded to ensure that the latest updates are accessible. For those interested in reviewing the evolution of this document or referencing previous iterations, they can find the old versions documented comprehensively in the gist history. The complete record of changes and historical versions is available at [this link](https://gist.github.com/ecdsa/dfa2d76a5fe845fd283c01b5ed12d274/). This ensures transparency and provides a resource for those who wish to see how the understanding and presentation of the UTXO commitment have evolved over time.
